hypothesis: If a conditional latent diffusion model is trained in the CIAE latent space with conditioning on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed element lists, then the generated shell-wise compositions will exhibit higher distributional similarity to real configurations (lower Fréchet Distance), better constraint compliance (lower Leakage), and higher point-wise quality (higher Cosine Similarity) than a chemically-agnostic baseline, and these advantages will scale with increasing training data because chemical conditioning provides priors that steer generation toward feasible, energetically plausible compositions.
